prompt:
We keep a tiny agent on each box that babysits one sidecar process, and the restart logic in it has been rewritten three times because nobody can test it — it's all real timers and real fork/exec. I want the decision core extracted into a pure state machine: package supervisor, root of module go-supervisor, stdlib only. No goroutines, no time.Sleep, no timers — the caller owns the event loop and the clock.

Shape:
- type Clock interface { Now() time.Time } — injected; all timestamps and backoff math come from it.
- type Process interface { Start() error } — the thing being supervised. Start is a synchronous launch attempt; an error means the launch failed. The supervisor never learns about exits on its own: the caller reports them.
- Policies: Always (restart on any exit), OnFailure (restart only on non-zero exit or failed launch), Never. Failed launches count as failures for policy purposes.
- Config: Process, Policy, BackoffBase (delay before the 1st restart, doubling each consecutive restart; 0 means immediate and stays 0), BackoffCap (upper bound on the delay, 0 = uncapped), StableAfter (a run lasting at least this long resets the doubling back to base; 0 = never reset), MaxRestarts + Window (give up when the budget of restarts within the trailing window is spent; MaxRestarts 0 = unlimited; a restart exactly Window ago has aged out). New(cfg, clock) must reject nonsense: nil process, unknown policy, MaxRestarts>0 with no Window (say "Window" in the error), cap below base.
- Driving it: Start() begins supervision (only from idle; it returns nil even when the first launch fails — that's a supervision event, not a caller error). ProcessExited(code) is only legal while running. Tick() performs at most one due restart, judged against clock.Now(). Stop() is the operator's off switch from running or backoff: cancels any pending restart, error in any other state. State() reports idle/running/backoff/stopped/done/given-up. PendingRestartAt() exposes the due time.
- Event log: Events() returns the ordered history — kinds "started", "start-failed" (with Err), "exited" (with Code), "restart-scheduled" (with Delay), "gave-up", "stopped", each stamped with the clock time. Returned slice must be a copy.

The acceptance tests (supervisor_test.go, protected) script a fake process and a fake clock through crash loops, launch failures, stable-run resets, budget exhaustion and operator stops — read them for the exact event sequences. go test -race -timeout 30s ./... must pass.
